title: Changing the Widget Type
breadcrumbs: Using Widgets in Forms and Reports
text: Changing the Widget Type Changing the widget type creates a (local) definition of a field that deviates from the application model default. Deviations of this sort do not cause errors, because at run time Uniface ignores any properties that the GUI (or widget type) does not recognize. Uniface uses the defaults of the project GUI for all undefined properties. Ensure that the values of all common widget properties are consistent for all widget types. You can then change widget types irrespective of style guidelines. For example, if Font specifies a suitable font for a list box field, you should be able to change the widget type of that field to another type that also recognizes the Font property, such as a radio group, without needing to adjust the font. Change the Widget Type property for the entity or field. If you change a widget's type after explicitly defining common properties for that widget, either in the application model or in the widget properties, the common properties are also defined for the new widget type. If you change a widget that has unique properties (for example, changing a meter to a progress bar) the properties of the old widget are still available in the More Properties dialog box. Related Links Widgets In Uniface, widgets are user interface controls. They can display data values, control how values are modified, and determine the appearance of those objects. Most widgets are field-level widgets. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
